children.heading: .—Determination of Need by the Agency
children.label: a .—Determination of Need by the Agency

children.text: there is a need for a rule;
there are a limited number of identifiable interests that will be significantly affected by the rule;

children.label: A
children.text: can adequately represent the interests identified under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: are willing to negotiate in good faith to reach a consensus on the proposed rule;
there is a reasonable likelihood that a committee will reach a consensus on the proposed rule within a fixed period of time;
the negotiated rulemaking procedure will not unreasonably delay the notice of proposed rulemaking and the issuance of the final rule;
the agency has adequate resources and is willing to commit such resources, including technical assistance, to the committee; and

the agency, to the maximum extent possible consistent with the legal obligations of the agency, will use the consensus of the committee with respect to the proposed rule as the basis for the rule proposed by the agency for notice and comment.

children.label: A
children.text: identifying persons who will be significantly affected by a proposed rule, including residents of rural areas; and

children.label: B
children.text: conducting discussions with such persons to identify the issues of concern to such persons, and to ascertain whether the establishment of a negotiated rulemaking committee is feasible and appropriate in the particular rulemaking.

children.heading: .—Duties of conveners
children.label: 2 .—Duties of conveners
children.text: The convener shall report findings and may make recommendations to the agency. Upon request of the agency, the convener shall ascertain the names of persons who are willing and qualified to represent interests that will be significantly affected by the proposed rule, including residents of rural areas. The report and any recommendations of the convener shall be made available to the public upon request.
